data.title: Pimco Explores Sale Of $14 Billion Oracle Data Center Debt
data.description:
Pacific Investment Management Co. is exploring a potential sale of a portion of the $14 billion in debt financing it is providing for a large-scale Oracle data center project, according to people familiar with the matter. The development in Saline Township, Michigan, is backed by $16.3 billion in total financing, with roughly 15% expected to come from equity and the remainder structured through bonds issued by a special purpose vehicle. Pimco is anchoring the transaction, which is being led by Bank of America and is expected to close on April 17, while Blackstone is planning to contribute about $2 billion in equity, based on previously reported details.

The financing structure could be notable for institutional investors assessing long-duration infrastructure exposure. The bonds carry a 19.5-year maturity and a 14-year weighted average life, with a six-year interest-only period followed by amortization over 13 years. The securities are being offered in a 144A format to large institutional investors, and Pimco is still evaluating demand as terms could evolve. Early pricing discussions suggest the bonds may offer slightly more than one percentage point above Oracle's existing April 2040 debt, which yielded around 6.5% on Wednesday, implying a coupon near 7.5% at current levels.

The transaction could also reflect Pimco's continued focus on large data center financings tied to growing demand for cloud and AI infrastructure. In a prior deal, the firm partnered with Blue Owl Capital to help finance Meta's Hyperion data center in Louisiana, where Pimco later moved to sell more than $1 billion of debt shortly after pricing, generating a reported $2 billion windfall for client funds. Whether a similar outcome emerges in this case could depend on investor appetite, final pricing, and broader conditions in credit markets.

data.title: TAP Reports 4.1 Million Profit As Lufthansa, Air France-KLM Submit Bids
data.description:
TAP SA reported a fourth consecutive year of profitability in 2025, though the earnings profile suggests some underlying noise as the airline advances both expansion plans and a potential strategic stake sale. Net income came in at 4.1 million, down from 53.7 million the prior year, with the decline attributed to a one-off corporate tax adjustment rather than core operating trends. Revenue increased 1.2% to 4.3 billion, while passenger traffic rose 3.4% to 16.7 million, indicating that demand remained relatively steady even as reported profit moved lower.

The update comes shortly after Deutsche Lufthansa AG (DLAKY) and Air France-KLM (AFLYY) submitted nonbinding bids for a stake in the carrier, as Portugal considers selling up to 49.9% of the business. IAG SA chose not to participate, stating that such an investment would not align with shareholder interests. The level of interest from major European airline groups could reflect TAP's network positioning, particularly its exposure to Brazil, Africa, and North America, which may be viewed as strategically valuable within broader consolidation efforts.

Management is also focused on improving efficiency and supporting future growth through fleet and network initiatives. TAP plans to modernize its fleet with Airbus NEO aircraft from Airbus, which could contribute to cost efficiencies over time. Growth is expected to be driven primarily by transatlantic routes, especially Brazil, alongside expanded operations from Porto, suggesting that the airline is leaning into markets where it already has established strength.

data.title: Aritzia Shares Jump 9.5% As Weekly Sales More Than Double
data.description:
Aritzia (ATZAF) shares moved sharply higher on Wednesday, rising 9.5% to C$124.99 and leading the S&P/TSX Composite, which closed up 1.2% in Toronto. The move appears to reflect both company-specific momentum and a shift in macro sentiment, as investors reacted to signs that tensions tied to the Iran conflict could ease. That dynamic may have helped lift consumer discretionary names more broadly, with the sector index gaining 3%, while Aritzia stood out as the only retailer among the top percentage gainers.

Recent transaction data is beginning to point to stronger underlying demand. Bloomberg Second Measure indicated that Aritzia's sales for the week ending March 29 more than doubled year over year, significantly ahead of peers such as Fast Retailing, which reported a 26.1% increase over the same period. Early indications suggest the spring collection is resonating with customers, particularly in the US market, which accounted for nearly 60% of revenue in the third quarter. Based on these trends, some analysts believe the company could be positioned to outperform expectations in its fiscal fourth-quarter 2026 results, expected in the coming weeks, if current momentum continues.

Positioning may also be resetting after a period of pressure. Shares had declined 5.5% since late February, as rising energy prices tied to the Iran conflict raised concerns around inflation and consumer spending. With potential de-escalation now in focus, that pressure on discretionary demand could begin to ease. At the same time, analysts are increasingly highlighting Aritzia's growth profile, particularly its expanding US presence, alongside continued balance sheet strength and cash accumulation, which may support flexibility as conditions evolve.

data.title: Middle-earth Meets North Texas as FAN EXPO Dallas Returns September 11-13, 2026
data.description:
Beloved Lord of the Rings stars and fan-favorite action icon headline a growing guest list for September's can't-miss pop culture event

DALLAS, TX / ACCESS Newswire / April 9, 2026 / FAN EXPO Dallas is turning up the excitement for its 2026 event with the addition of Karl Urban to an already unforgettable celebrity lineup. Best known for his roles in The Lord of the Rings, Star Trek, The Boys, and Dredd, Urban joins a previously announced reunion of the beloved Hobbits from Middle-earth, making this year's event a must-attend for fans of fantasy, sci-fi, and beyond.

Taking place September 11-13, 2026, at the Kay Bailey Hutchison Convention Center in Dallas, FAN EXPO Dallas will bring together fans from across the country for three days of celebrity appearances, panels, autographs, cosplay, and immersive fan experiences.

Urban joins an iconic Hobbit lineup featuring:

  • Elijah Wood (Frodo Baggins)

  • Sean Astin (Samwise Gamgee)

  • Dominic Monaghan (Meriadoc Brandybuck)

  • Billy Boyd (Peregrin Took)

Together, these fan-favorite stars represent one of the most beloved ensembles in cinematic history, offering attendees the rare opportunity to celebrate the enduring legacy of The Lord of the Rings.

"Karl Urban's addition to this year's lineup takes an already incredible guest roster to the next level," said Andrew Moyes, Vice President of FAN EXPO HQ. "Combined with the Hobbits reunion, we're creating an experience that taps into the heart of fandom and delivers unforgettable moments for attendees."

Urban portrayed Éomer, Marshal of the Riddermark, in The Lord of the Rings trilogy, making his appearance alongside the Hobbits especially meaningful for fans eager to relive the magic of Middle-earth.

This announcement marks just the beginning of what promises to be one of the most exciting FAN EXPO Dallas events to date. Organizers confirm that more celebrity guests, programming details, and exclusive experiences will be announced in the coming months, continuing to build momentum leading into September.

FAN EXPO Dallas brings together fans across every corner of pop culture, creating an energetic space where shared interests turn into real connections.

"FAN EXPO Dallas goes beyond what people expect from a convention. It's an experience where fans can fully immerse themselves in the stories, characters, and communities they love," said Andrew Moyes. "Whether you're a lifelong collector, a first-time attendee, or coming with family, there's a sense of excitement and belonging that makes the event truly special for all ages."

At the heart of the event is a robust lineup of programming designed to bring fandoms to life. Attendees can enjoy behind-the-scenes panels, live entertainment, themed events, and exclusive content tied to some of the biggest franchises in film and television. The convention floor also transforms into a visual spectacle, with cosplay taking center stage as fans debut intricate, character-inspired creations that highlight the creativity and passion of the community.

To explore how to get involved in FAN EXPO Dallas 2026:

  • Community Zone

  • Cosplay Guests

  • Fan Panels

  • Masters of Cosplay Competition

  • Itasha Showcase

  • Idolfest

  • Cosplay Showcase

To see the guest list, planned events and to purchase tickets, go to www.fanexpodallas.com

WHEN: FAN EXPO Dallas runs Friday, September 11 through Sunday, September 13, 2026

Friday, September 11, from 4:00 P.M. to 9:00 P.M.

Saturday, September 12, from 10:00 A.M. to 7:00 P.M.

Sunday, September 13, from 10:00 A.M. to 5:00 P.M.

WHERE:

Kay Bailey Hutchison Convention Center located at 650 S. Griffin St., Dallas, TX 75202

COST:

Advance Full-price one day tickets start at $33.

Advanced price 3-day passes start at $99 and full-price 3-day passes start at $115. Ultimate packages advance prices start at $139 and full-price Ultimate packages start at $155. VIP Packages start at $519 and are only available in advance.

Hours and information are available at fanexpodallas.com.

ABOUT: With over a million fans and counting, FAN EXPO HQ attracts pop culture enthusiasts, locally and internationally, to come together to celebrate all things fandom. Collectively, it hosts more than one million fans annually at FAN EXPO Dallas™, MEGACON Orlando, FAN EXPO Canada™, FAN EXPO Boston™, FAN EXPO Denver, FAN EXPO San Francisco, CALGARY EXPO, FAN EXPO Chicago, FAN EXPO Philadelphia, FAN EXPO Portland, FAN EXPO New Orleans, FAN EXPO Cleveland, FAN EXPO Vancouver™, Toronto Comicon, Dallas FAN FESTIVAL, FAN EXPO Anaheim: Special Edition, VidCon and EDMONTON EXPO. The latest schedule of events is available here on their website, along with up-to-date ticket information. Discover. Celebrate. Belong.

###

data.title: Guardian Roofing, Gutters & Insulation Teams with KATU's "Everyday Heroes" to Honor Portland Community Changemakers Through the HALO Project
data.description:
PORTLAND, OR / ACCESS Newswire / April 9, 2026 / Guardian Roofing, Gutters & Insulation is honored to participate in Everyday Heroes, an ongoing initiative from KATU that recognizes volunteers and nonprofit organizations whose compassion and commitment are helping build a stronger Portland community.

Everyday Heroes highlights individuals and groups who selflessly give their time, energy, and resources to serve others, often without recognition. Through this collaboration, Guardian Roofing will support KATU in uplifting stories that reflect the spirit of service, resilience, and generosity found throughout the region.

"Everyday Heroes reminds us that the strength of a community is measured by the people who show up for others," said Mat Rzucidlo, President of Guardian Roofing, Gutters & Insulation. "It is a privilege to help recognize those whose kindness and dedication make a lasting difference in the lives of their neighbors."

The initiative also aligns with Guardian Roofing's ongoing commitment to community care through its HALO Project Roof Giveaway, an annual program that provides new roofs at no cost to deserving homeowners facing financial hardship. Many HALO recipients are individuals who have spent years serving others, including veterans, caregivers, volunteers, and nonprofit leaders, and now find themselves in need of support.

"The HALO Project is rooted in gratitude," Rzucidlo said. "It is our way of giving back to people who have given so much of themselves to their communities. A safe roof brings comfort, stability, and peace of mind, and we are honored to help provide that when it is needed most."

Throughout the nomination period, KATU will continue to feature Everyday Heroes across its broadcast and digital platforms, sharing stories that inspire connection and community involvement. One nominated hero will also be selected in connection with the HALO Project Roof Giveaway, extending the initiative's impact from recognition to meaningful, tangible support.

"Nominating an Everyday Hero is a simple act of appreciation that can have a profound effect," Rzucidlo said. "It tells people that their efforts are seen, valued, and deeply appreciated."

Nominations open April 9, 2026 and close July 29, 2026. Community members are encouraged to nominate individuals who generously give their time and talents to serve others - whether that's a local volunteer, a veteran continuing to support their community, or an everyday neighbor, making Portland a more caring and connected place to live.

For more information or to submit a nomination, visit KATU's Everyday Heroes page.

About Guardian Roofing, Gutters & Insulation

Founded in 2005, Guardian Roofing, Gutters & Insulation is more than a home services company - it's a team built on trust, craftsmanship, and community. With offices in Seattle and Portland, Guardian has grown into one of the Pacific Northwest's most recognized roofing providers, known for its integrity, safety, and dedication to doing the right thing-always. The company's award-winning culture emphasizes professional growth, teamwork, and service to others through initiatives like the HALO Project, which provides free roofs to deserving families. As Guardian continues its expansion into new markets in 2026, it remains committed to its founding promise: to protect homes, strengthen communities, and deliver service that stands the test of time.

For more information, visit www.GuardianRoofing.com. Licenses: WA UBI 604-059-205 | FEIN #81-4469822

Media Contact:

Dana Cobb

The Barber Shop Marketing

dana@thebarbershopmarketing.com

972.955.9747

SOURCE: Guardian Roofing

data.title: CleanGo Clarifies Proposed Amendment of Warrants to Include Repricing and Extension
data.description:
(TheNewswire)

    VANCOUVER, BC – April 9, 2026 -TheNewswire – CleanGo Innovations Inc. (CSE: CGII; OTCQB: CLGOF; FRA:APO.F), (“CleanGo” or the“Company”) wishes to clarify its priordisclosure regarding the proposed amendment of an aggregate of1,000,000 common share purchase warrants (the “Warrants”), issuedin connection with convertible note conversions on April 18, 2023 andJune 26, 2023, and a debt settlement on June 6, 2023, which arecurrently set to expire on April 18, June 6, and June 26, 2026,respectively.

    The Company announces that it intends to amend theterms of the Warrants, subject to approval of the Canadian SecuritiesExchange, to (i) extend the expiry date of the Warrants to March 31,2028, and (ii) reprice the exercise price of the Warrants from $0.40per share to $0.57 per share.

    All other terms and conditions of the Warrants willremain unchanged.

    The Company believes that the proposed amendments willprovide warrant holders with additional flexibility while aligning theexercise price with current market conditions and supporting theCompany’s capital structure.

    About CleanGo Innovations Inc.

    CleanGo Innovations Inc. (CSE: CGII; OTCQB: CLGOF; FRA:APO) is a leading developer and manufacturer of proprietaryGreen solutions in the residential, oil and gas, marine, and industrialcleaning markets, utilizing green chemistry andcommercial infrastructure certified green,non-toxic, and sustainable cleaning and industrial solutions. With afocus on innovation and environmental responsibility, CleanGo offers adiverse portfolio of products for retail, commercial, and industrialapplications, including the demanding oil and gas servicessector.

    This joint venture underscores CleanGo Innovations’commitment to global expansion and its dedication to fostering acleaner, greener future for critical industries worldwide.

    For more information, visitwww.cleangoinnovations.com.

    CleanGo Innovations Inc.

    Anthony Sarvucci

    President/CEO

    This news release contains “forward-lookinginformation” within the meaning of applicable Canadian securitieslegislation. Forward-looking information includes, but is not limitedto, statements regarding the proposed amendment of the Warrants,including the extension of the expiry date and repricing of theexercise price, and the receipt of approval from the CanadianSecurities Exchange. Forward-looking information is based on a numberof assumptions, including that all necessary regulatory approvals willbe obtained and that market conditions will remain stable.

    Forward-looking information is subject to known andunknown risks, uncertainties and other factors that may cause actualresults to differ materially from those expressed or implied in suchinformation, including, without limitation, the risk that the proposedamendments may not be completed as anticipated or at all, and thatrequired approvals may not be obtained in a timely manner or atall.

data.title: Amazon's Plan To Cut Support For Older Kindle Devices Draws Criticism
data.description:
SEATTLE (dpa-AFX) - Starting May 20, 2026, Amazon is set to discontinue support for older Kindle devices released in 2012 or earlier, and this has sparked frustration among loyal users.

With this change, devices affected-including early versions like the Kindle Touch and the first-gen Kindle Fire tablets-won't be able to buy, download, or borrow new e-books from the Kindle Store anymore. But on the bright side, users can still read the e-books they've already downloaded.

Amazon explained that these devices have received support for up to 18 years, but they can't keep up with the latest features and security needs. To make the transition easier, the company is offering discounts to entice users to upgrade to newer models.

However, some users are not happy about this. They argue that their older Kindles still work fine and don't really need constant updates. There are also worries about the environmental impact, with estimates suggesting that as many as 2 million devices might become obsolete.

Industry experts note that while the decision makes sense considering the limitations of aging hardware, it does risk turning fully functional devices into little more than offline readers.

This raises more questions about sustainability and the future support for gadgets.

Copyright(c) 2026 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

data.title: Cogeco Communications Inc reports results for the quarter ended February 28 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Cogeco Communications Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of C$1.86​​ per share for the quarter ended February 28, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of C$1.76. The mean expectation of nine analysts for the quarter was for earnings of C$2.06 per share. Wall Street expected results to range from C$1.97 to C$2.23 per share.

  • Revenue fell 5.3% to C$693.56 million from a year ago; analysts expected C$699.25 million.

  • Cogeco Communications Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was C$1.89​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of C$80.01 million.

  • Cogeco Communications Inc shares had fallen by 3.5% this quarter and gained 2.5% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 0.2% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, one analyst negatively revised an earnings estimate

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is 3 "strong buy" or "buy," 6 "hold" and 1 "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the integrated telecommunications services peer group is also "hold"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Cogeco Communications Inc is C$74.13, about 10% above its last closing price of C$66.70

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data April 9 at 10:35 p.m. UTC. ​All figures in Canadian dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: Intel Announces Google Commitment To Future Xeon Chips In AI Push
data.description:
Intel is attempting to reassert its position in data center infrastructure, with Alphabet's Google committing to adopt future generations of its Xeon processors as part of a newly announced multiyear agreement. The partnership also includes plans for Google to customize Intel's infrastructure processing units, which are designed to manage networking, security, and storage functions. While financial terms were not disclosed, the agreement could signal a deeper level of integration between the two companies as demand for AI-related compute continues to expand.

The development comes as Intel works through a broader effort to regain traction in data centers after ceding share to competitors such as Advanced Micro Devices and to internal silicon programs developed by customers including Google. Xeon processors previously held more than 99% market share and served as a primary profit driver, but that position has weakened in recent years. Under CEO Lip-Bu Tan, Intel appears focused on capturing a larger portion of AI infrastructure spending, with its CPUs potentially positioned as complementary components within increasingly complex system architectures.

That shift could align with evolving AI workloads, where CPUs are being used to orchestrate training environments and support inference tasks alongside specialized accelerators like those from Nvidia . Google's AI infrastructure leadership indicated confidence in Intel's Xeon roadmap to meet performance and efficiency requirements, with both companies highlighting a more balanced system design aimed at improving utilization and reducing complexity. For investors, while near-term financial impact remains undisclosed, the agreement could suggest Intel is beginning to rebuild its role within next-generation AI data center deployments.

data.title: New Zealand March seasonally adjusted PMI 53.2 -Business NZ/Bank of NZ survey
data.description:
New Zealand's manufacturing index fell in March but continued to expand, a survey showed on Friday.

The Bank of New Zealand-Business NZ's seasonally adjusted Performance of Manufacturing Index stood at 53.2 in March, compared with 54.8 in February.

A reading above 50 indicates manufacturing activity is expanding, while anything below that threshold points to contraction.

BusinessNZ’s Director of Advocacy Catherine Beard said in a statement while it was gratifying to see the manufacturing sector was still expanding, it was concerning the number of businesses commenting negatively about their situation had increased.

data.title: Oracle Launches Agentic Apps for Enterprise Workflows
data.description:
Oracle (ORCL, Financials) rolled out a new set of agentic applications for enterprise customers, expanding its artificial intelligence strategy across finance, supply chain management, human resources and customer relations.

Oracle's Fusion Cloud Applications now come with a new software bundle dubbed Fusion Agentic Applications. The company is marketing the tools as a method for businesses to automate jobs, make better decisions, and make day-to-day operations run more smoothly in all of their essential roles.

The release is important because it shows that Oracle is going beyond cloud infrastructure and into applied AI, where big software companies are trying to make their platforms more useful and tougher to replace. Oracle is also making it easier for current customers to use AI by adding these technologies to existing Fusion products instead of making them buy new ones.

This is part of a larger drive by Oracle to improve its corporate software at a time when businesses are searching for practical uses of AI that can boost productivity and cut down on human labor. It also backs up Oracle's bigger claim that its cloud platform can manage both the infrastructure and the apps layer of AI adoption.

Investors will probably keep an eye on how soon customers start using the new capabilities and whether they help cloud apps grow in the next few quarters.

data.title: Samsung Plans $4 Billion Vietnam Chip Packaging Plant Expansion
data.description:
Samsung Electronics (SSNLF) is exploring another sizable step-up in its Vietnam strategy, with plans for a $4 billion chip packaging plant in Thai Nguyen province, according to people familiar with the matter. The investment is expected to be phased, beginning with an initial $2 billion allocation, while Vietnam's finance ministry said it is working on a memorandum of understanding tied to a semiconductor project, without outlining details. The timing aligns with a broader industry push, as chipmakers scale capacity to meet rising demand tied to artificial intelligence workloads across data centers and consumer devices.

Vietnam could become even more central to Samsung's global manufacturing footprint as this project develops. The company entered the country in 2008 with a facility in Bac Ninh and has since built out what is now its largest smartphone production base, including a major complex in Thai Nguyen launched in 2013. At the same time, global supply chains have been gradually diversifying away from China, partly to navigate trade tensions and tariff pressures under US President Donald Trump's policies, with Vietnam maintaining strong export momentum and emerging as a preferred alternative for electronics manufacturing.

Samsung's role in that shift has been substantial, with total investment in Vietnam exceeding $23.2 billion and around 90,000 jobs supported as of 2024. The company has continued to scale its Thai Nguyen presence, including a $920 million investment in 2022 that lifted capital at its Samsung Electro-Mechanics facility to $2.3 billion, alongside a separate $1.2 billion commitment earlier this year for high-end circuit board production. The proposed chip packaging plant could mark a further move into higher-value semiconductor activity, potentially reinforcing Vietnam's position within the global electronics supply chain.

data.title: Prologis And La Caisse Launch 1 Billion European Logistics Venture
data.description:
Caisse de Depot et Placement du Quebec is moving deeper into European logistics at a time when market conditions remain uneven, partnering with Prologis Inc. to establish a 1 billion ($1.2 billion) joint venture focused on acquiring and operating assets across key markets including France, Germany, the Netherlands, Sweden and the UK. The structure gives La Caisse a 70% stake, with both parties indicating that the platform could expand over time through additional acquisitions and development. The strategy appears anchored in the view that logistics real estate may continue to benefit from structural demand drivers, even as near-term volatility persists.

That backdrop, however, is becoming more complex. The conflict in the Middle East has introduced fresh uncertainty into commercial real estate just as pricing and transaction activity had been recovering from the 2022 inflation shock. At the same time, higher bond yields often used as a proxy for property valuations have started to pressure deal economics and may affect transactions that were previously agreed. Within logistics, rising fuel costs could weigh on corporate margins, which in turn may soften rental demand, suggesting that the near-term outlook for the sector is not without friction.

Even so, both La Caisse and Prologis appear focused on longer-term fundamentals that could continue to support the asset class. Growth in ecommerce and ongoing shifts in manufacturing including efforts to bring production closer to end markets are expected to sustain demand for logistics space. In parallel, elevated financing costs may discourage speculative development, potentially limiting new supply unless pre-leasing is secured. With construction levels already relatively low in several markets, that dynamic could tighten supply-demand conditions over time, particularly if tenants continue making leasing decisions despite the current uncertainty.

data.title: Kia Unveils 2027 SDV Plan, Targets 2029 Robot Deployment, Cuts EV Goal To 1 Million
data.description:
Kia Corp. (KIMTF) is stepping deeper into next-generation mobility, outlining a dual-track strategy that combines software-defined vehicles with automation in manufacturing. The affiliate of Hyundai Motor Co. said it expects to complete development of its first software-defined vehicle by the end of 2027, equipped with Level 2 driving automation for highway use, with a more advanced version capable of operating on regular roads targeted for early 2029. In parallel, Kia plans to introduce the Atlas humanoid robotdeveloped by Boston Dynamicsinto its Georgia facility in 2029, initially handling simpler production tasks before potentially expanding into more complex processes through AI-based learning.

The broader backdrop suggests automakers are increasingly leaning into software and efficiency to navigate a shifting demand environment, particularly in electric vehicles. Software-defined vehicles are emerging as a sizable opportunity, with estimates pointing to a market currently valued at around $250 billion to $300 billion that could exceed $1 trillion over the next decade. Within that context, Kia's transition could allow it to generate recurring revenue through software subscriptions across a vehicle's lifecycle, which may support higher per-unit profitability while lowering repair-related costs. To reinforce this direction, the company plans to invest more than $500 million into physical AI and vision-language-action models, while expanding partnerships with technology players including DeepMind and Nvidia Corp..

At the same time, Kia is recalibrating its volume expectations as it adjusts to evolving market dynamics. The company lowered its 2030 EV sales target to 1 million units from 1.26 million previously, while increasing its hybrid ambition to 1.1 million units across 13 models. Its total global sales outlook now stands at 4.13 million units, down slightly from 4.19 million, with plans to sell 1 million units in the U.S. and 1.5 million units in emerging markets, including a focus on India. Taken together, the strategy suggests Kia is positioning for a more diversified growth profile, balancing software, automation, and hybrid expansion as industry conditions continue to evolve.

data.title: ConocoPhillips visits Venezuela to evaluate oil opportunities
data.description:
Key points:
  • ConocoPhillips sends team to Venezuela after 2007 nationalization forced its exit
  • Exxon Mobil also recently sent a team to assess Venezuelan oil sector
  • US President Trump has urged oil firms to help rebuild Venezuela's energy industry

By Sheila Dang

ConocoPhillips said on Thursday it is sending a small evaluation team to Venezuela this week to evaluate oil and gas opportunities.

The U.S. oil producer left the South American country in 2007 after its assets there were nationalized. The company is owed about $12 billion with interest following arbitration awards stemming from the expropriations.

ConocoPhillips said it will evaluate potential new Venezuela investments as it works to collect what it is owed.

U.S. President Donald Trump has urged oil firms to spend billions and rebuild Venezuela's energy sector after American forces removed President Nicolas Maduro in January. Top U.S. oil major Exxon Mobil - which also exited the country in 2007 - sent a technical team last month to evaluate the infrastructure and investment opportunities.

ConocoPhillips CEO Ryan Lance said last month that Venezuela needed to "completely rewire" ​its fiscal system to attract new investment and called recent reforms "woefully inadequate."
